steps.prompt_snapshot:
Super-Eywa v1 runtime prompt snapshot.
Turn index: 1
Node depth: 0
Allowed decisions: ['review']
Prompt family: review
Base header prompt: ''
Selected prompt text:
Write a tentative answer, then send one reviewer child a focused request to critique it, verify it, or improve it before the final answer is produced.
The reviewer child should help stress-test the draft, not wander into unrelated subproblems.
Use plain text only inside the draft and reviewer message. Do not use markdown, code fences, or LaTeX-style backslashes.
Keep the JSON compact.
Keep decision_notes extremely short. Prefer 3 to 8 words.
Make draft_response exactly two short lines:
FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Keep message_for_reviewer to two short sentences or fewer.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "review",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "draft_response": "plain-text draft answer",
  "message_for_reviewer": "plain-text review request for the child"
}

Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-B1-usaco-2024-us-open-platinum-identity-theft
Title: B1. USACO 2024 US Open Platinum / Identity Theft

Problem:
Extend cows' bitstring IDs so no cow can be mistaken for another when some reported IDs are truncated.

Grading target:
Binary hidden-test acceptance.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

authored_response.decision_notes: Drafting logic for bitstring extension problem
authored_response.draft_response:
FINAL_ANSWER: The problem requires finding a minimal extension such that no prefix of one ID matches another. This is solved by constructing a Trie of existing IDs and finding the shortest path to a leaf in the complement tree.
JUSTIFICATION: Uses Trie-based prefix analysis to ensure unique identification.
authored_response.message_for_reviewer: Verify if the Trie-based approach correctly handles the minimal extension requirement. Check if the logic accounts for all prefix-collision cases.
